description: Lets the logged-in twinme.me web app open the system browser for Google sign-in (Google blocks OAuth inside the embedded webview). The open_external command is Rust-scoped to twinme.me URLs only, so this remote origin can only launch our OWN auth URLs in the browser — never an arbitrary site or a local program. Kept separate from twinme-auth-bridge so that capability stays single-purpose (the keyring token).
//windows: MAIN window only — the remote TwinMe app loads there. The hummingbird overlay never signs in, so it is excluded.
//remote: Same remote scope as the auth-token bridge: the twinme.me apex + its https subdomains, nothing else.
//permissions: ONLY the autogenerated app-command permission for open_external (declared in build.rs AppManifest + generate_handler!). No core/plugin APIs are exposed to the remote origin through this capability.
